mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in >50% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0, or the signal's predictive R-squared drops below 0.01 over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint rise in housing starts and revolving consumer credit signals a synchronized expansion in durable goods demand and household liquidity, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST and RSAFS z-scores) shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (p > 0.10), or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in 3 consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 99.0, supply-side capacity constraints emerge, leading to input cost inflation that compresses corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of cases where INDPRO breaches 99.0, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth accelerating above 2.5% annualized confirms robust aggregate demand, which lifts equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of triggered windows, or the information coefficient between GDPC1 growth surprises and 5-day SPX returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and CPI readings above 230 indicate a wage-price feedback loop that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy faster than priced in, causing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/CPIAUCSL signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in >60% of co-occurring high-value instances, or the interaction term's t-statistic remains below 1.5 over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.25% while the CPI index remains above 230, the market prices in a hawkish policy pivot to combat sticky inflation, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% with CPIAUCSL above 230
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >50% of instances where both thresholds are breached, or the signal's Sharpe ratio over a 3-year out-of-sample period falls below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in revolving consumer credit reflects sustained household liquidity and spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and SPX forward returns remains below 0.05 for any 12-month period, or the signal's hit rate drops below 50% in a walk-forward test.
